headline: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
description: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

headline: At the G7, the US wants no mention of the words 'climate change'
Israel faces European backlash over Gaza flotilla detentions
How Italian authorities hamper the work of NGOs rescuing migrants
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (3/4). Since Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni took office in 2022, organizations providing rescue services to migrants have faced increasing difficulty operating.

headline: US indicts former Cuban president as pressure builds
description: An indictment unsealed in a federal court in Florida charged Raul Castro over the 1996 downing of two civilian planes manned by anti-Castro pilots. The charges came as Washington called on the communist-ruled island's people to embrace a 'new path.'

headline: Jeremy D. Popkin, historian: 'Debates on slavery began decades before the French Revolution'
description: The American scholar traces the legal and intellectual history of abolitionism in France, showing how the question of slavery influenced the trajectories of many leading figures of the revolutionary movement.

headline: 'France's president-PM diarchy is shaping the strategy of many 2027 hopefuls'
description: The role of prime minister has regained central importance since 2022 and the end of outright majorities in the Assemblée Nationale, observes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

headline: US needs to 'put its footprint back on Greenland,' says envoy Landry
French lawmakers back Macron choice to run central bank
Former Arafat adviser faces deportation from France
EU approves trade deal with Washington and sets safeguards
'A more arbitrary and extreme regime': In the West Bank, Israel toughens death penalty law for Palestinians
'Heat strokes are part of the job': In Delhi, construction workers endure 43°C heat
Historic buildings in Normandy's Honfleur on verge of collapse
Robin Campillo, filmmaker: 'I don't see how a blacklist of French cinema could be enforced. Would it even be legal?'
Studios Architecture Paris and Selldorf Architects selected to lead Louvre transformation
French presidential candidate Philippe under investigation for embezzlement
Co-leader of German far right hits out at France's Bardella
Cannes 2026: Pedro Almodovar's 'Bitter Christmas' explores writing and self-reflection
Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

headline: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
description: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.

headline: Ebola outbreak in the DRC: West's aid cuts 'contributed to the weakening of the surveillance chain'
description: In an interview, the head of Africa CDC, the African Union's health agency, denounces the impact that drastic reductions in international aid have had on the delayed management of the ongoing outbreak.

Canal+ controversy undermines French cinema
